name: Prose: "A Hunter Turned Naturalist" — Jim Corbett

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - dedicated MCQ set (6 questions); brainkart.com - Champawat Tiger details and National Park legacy

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'hunter turned naturalist/jim corbett' returned zero hits — web research only. A biographical piece about Edward James (Jim) Corbett, who hunted man-eating tigers/leopards only at government request while genuinely admiring wildlife — distinct from sport hunting — and who later became a key figure in establishing India's first national park.

reading.intro: A biography of Jim Corbett — hunter, tracker, wildlife photographer, naturalist, and writer — who hunted man-eating tigers and leopards only at the Government's request to protect people, while genuinely admiring wildlife and refusing to hunt for sport.

qa.question: What was Jim Corbett famous for, and what was his dog's name?
qa.answer: Wildlife photography and hunting. His dog's name was Robin.

qa.question: What kind of animals did Jim Corbett hunt, and under what circumstances?
qa.answer: Man-eating tigers and leopards — and ONLY at the request of the Government, to help protect people, not for sport or pleasure.

qa.question: What was Corbett's attitude toward wildlife in general, despite being an expert hunter?
qa.answer: He admired wildlife and did not like to kill wild animals for pleasure or sport — but he did not hesitate to kill specifically man-eating tigers or leopards when needed to protect human lives.

qa.question: According to Corbett, when does a tiger typically become a man-eater, and what happens once a tiger has eaten human flesh?
qa.answer: A tiger typically becomes a man-eater when it gets OLD OR INJURED (and can no longer hunt its usual prey effectively). Once a tiger has eaten human flesh, it will NOT go back to eating other prey — it continues preying on humans.

qa.question: What was the Champawat Tiger, and what did Corbett's post-mortem reveal about it?
qa.answer: A Bengal tigress responsible for nearly 436 deaths across Nepal and the Kumaon area of India, shot by Corbett in 1907. The post-mortem revealed her upper and lower canine teeth on the right side were broken — an old injury from a previous gunshot by another hunter who had failed to track and kill her, which likely impaired her ability to hunt normal prey and turned her toward man-eating.

qa.question: What is Jim Corbett's lasting legacy regarding India's national parks?
qa.answer: He played a key role in establishing India's FIRST national park in the Kumaon Hills (originally named Hailey National Park, in the 1930s, in present-day Uttarakhand). It was renamed in 1957 as Jim Corbett National Park, in his honour. He died on 19 April 1955.

options: It will not go back to eating other prey
questions.explanation: It will not go back to eating other prey.

options: A broken leg from a trap
Broken canine teeth from a previous gunshot
questions.explanation: Broken canine teeth from a previous gunshot.

questions.prompt: Which national park, established with Jim Corbett's involvement, was later renamed in his honour (1957)?
options: Hailey National Park (now Jim Corbett National Park)
questions.explanation: Hailey National Park, renamed Jim Corbett National Park.
